fix: use original image for AI vision path, not binarized version

GPT-4o vision works better with natural color/grayscale images.
The binarization pipeline (adaptive threshold) was designed for
Tesseract OCR and distorts digits/letters when applied to images
sent to the vision model — causing misreads that look like invented data.

- Add preprocessForAI(): resize only (max 2048px), JPEG 0.95, no
  grayscale/binarization/deskew — preserves original color and detail
- callParseInvoiceAI(): use preprocessForAI instead of preprocessForOCR

* fix: add label verification, error logging, and workflow-run fallback for validation completion detection

Bug 1: validate_process.sh set_tracking_phase_label now logs errors instead

was actually applied and retries with direct add if missing.

validation phase handler. When ai:validated label is missing but the last
validation workflow run concluded success, applies the label and marks
complete.

dispatch_validation_if_needed to prevent redispatching when a validation
run is still in progress despite the stale threshold being reached.

commit.message:
Fix stuck orchestrate poller when wave has uncreated issues with no pending definitions (#716)

When wave issues have github_issue == null and no pending_issue_defs exist,
the deferred creation mechanism cannot create them. Previously, the poller
would loop forever: check-wave-status reported wave_complete=false (because
not_created entries block all_merged), stall detection skipped null entries,
and the judge only ran on wave_complete=true.

Now the poller detects this stuck state after terminal status persistence:
if all created (non-null) issues are terminal and remaining null issues
have no pending definitions, it invokes the judge to resolve the situation
(e.g., define missing issues, skip them, or take corrective action).
The judge prompt includes explicit context about the stuck wave.

commit.message:
refactor: tech debt consolidation — Phases 1-4 (#409)

* docs: add tech debt audit report and implementation plan

Comprehensive codebase audit covering frontend (21 views, 12 stores, CSS),
backend (6 Rust crates), and cross-cutting concerns. Reviewed by Opus 4.6,
GPT 5.4, and Codex 5.3. Includes prioritized 4-phase implementation plan
with 25 tasks.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: align feature flag defaults between Rust and TypeScript

Feature flags export_view, mcp_servers, and skills had different default
values in Rust (config.rs) vs TypeScript (defaults.ts/preferences.ts).
This caused users to see different feature availability depending on
whether Rust or TS defaults loaded first.

- Extract DEFAULT_FEATURES constant as single source of truth in TS
- Align TS defaults to match Rust: exportView=false, mcpServers=true, skills=true
- Preferences store now derives initial values from DEFAULT_FEATURES
- Hydration fallbacks use spread instead of per-field ?? operators

Fixes tech-debt-consolidation-report.md §7.2

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: persist repo scope across skills store mutations

loadSkills(repoRoot?) accepted an optional repo filter, but 9 mutation
functions called loadSkills() without it after create/update/rename/
import operations. This caused the skills list to reload with all
skills instead of the scoped view.

Store now persists currentRepoRoot internally and uses it as the default
for subsequent reloads. Callers can still override by passing a new
repoRoot explicitly.

Fixes tech-debt-consolidation-report.md §3.6

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: simplify useAsyncData by removing retry/backoff/resetOnExecute

Strip unused features from useAsyncData composable (0 production
consumers). Remove retry with exponential backoff, canRetry ref,
resetOnExecute option, and associated internal state.

Keep core API: data, loading, error, execute, refresh, clearError,
reset. Stale request prevention via useAsyncGuard is preserved.

332 → 101 lines source, 724 → 368 lines tests (8 test cases removed).

Implements tech-debt-consolidation-report.md §2.1 / Task 1.3

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add PageShell component and migrate 4 views

Add minimal PageShell to @tracepilot/ui — wraps content in the
standard .page-content > .page-content-inner layout with a fluid
prop for full-bleed layouts. Includes test suite.

Migrate NotFoundView, AnalyticsDashboardView, SessionDetailView,
ExportView, and ToolAnalysisView from raw div wrappers to PageShell.
17 more views remain for future migration.

Implements tech-debt-consolidation-report.md §1.1 / Task 1.4

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add runAction/runMutation store helpers and migrate skills store

- Create runAction<T> for query operations (loading+error+guard+action)
- Create runMutation<T> for CUD operations (error+action, returns T|null)
- Add AsyncGuardLike interface for guard compatibility
- Migrate loadSkills, createSkill, deleteSkill in skills store
- Fix test mock to pass through real runAction/runMutation from @tracepilot/ui
- 12 unit tests for helpers, all 1918 frontend tests pass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: migrate 20 raw stat-card divs to StatCard component

Migrate analytics views to use the shared StatCard component from @tracepilot/ui:
- HealthScoringView: 4 cards (success/warning/danger colors)
- CodeImpactView: 3 of 4 cards (1 kept raw for custom gradient class)
- AnalyticsDashboardView: 5 cards (incl. gradient + tooltip props)
- ModelComparisonView: 4 cards
- ToolAnalysisView: 4 cards

Reduces 80+ lines of repeated stat-card markup to declarative props.
One card (lines-added-value) retained as raw div due to custom gradient CSS.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: extract with_task_db helper to eliminate repeated mutex boilerplate

Add with_task_db() async helper that encapsulates the get_or_init → spawn_blocking →
lock → unwrap Option pattern. Migrate 9 simple task CRUD commands to use it:
- task_create_batch, task_get, task_list, task_cancel, task_retry
- task_delete, task_stats, task_list_jobs, task_cancel_job

Complex commands (task_create, orchestration) retain direct get_or_init_task_db
usage due to additional state requirements.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: extract Tauri IPC event names into shared constants

Replace 16 hardcoded event name strings across Rust and TypeScript with
shared constants:
- Rust: crate::events module with 6 constants
- TS: IPC_EVENTS object in @tracepilot/types
- Updated 2 Rust files (search.rs, helpers.rs) and 2 TS files
  (useIndexingEvents.ts, search.ts)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: replace legacy CSS tokens with canonical design system tokens

Replace 29 instances of undefined --color-*, --bg-*, and bare --accent
tokens with their canonical design-tokens.css equivalents:
- --color-fg-default → --text-primary
- --color-fg-muted → --text-secondary
- --color-canvas-default → --canvas-default
- --color-canvas-subtle → --canvas-subtle
- --color-accent-subtle → --accent-subtle

commit.message:
fix(middleware): add per-tool-type frequency detection to LoopDetectionMiddleware (#1988)

* fix(middleware): add per-tool-type frequency detection to LoopDetectionMiddleware

The existing hash-based loop detection only catches identical tool call
sets. When the agent calls the same tool type (e.g. read_file) on many
different files, each call produces a unique hash and bypasses detection.
This causes the agent to exhaust recursion_limit, consuming 150K-225K
tokens per failed run.

Add a second detection layer that tracks cumulative call counts per tool
type per thread. Warns at 30 calls (configurable) and forces stop at 50.
The hard stop message now uses the actual returned message instead of a
hardcoded constant, so both hash-based and frequency-based stops produce
accurate diagnostics.

Also fix _apply() to use the warning message returned by
_track_and_check() for hard stops, instead of always using _HARD_STOP_MSG.

Closes #1987

* Apply suggestions from code review

* SM-19: Add singleCandidate tail fallback for member calls with unresolvable receiver type

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus/sessions/469eac38-b0c0-4a26-a2ff-3eb06299730b

Co-authored-by: magyargergo <11230420+magyargergo@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(SM-19): address all PR #770 review findings + fix CI

Fixes all 5 test failures (2 unit + 3 integration) and addresses 10
review findings from comment 4225312416.

Critical fix — singleCandidate null-route guard
The SM-19 dispatcher chained singleCandidate as an unconditional tail
fallback for member calls with receiverTypeName. This bypassed the
SM-10 R3 null-route contract: when the receiver type IS in the index
but file/owner filtering produced zero matches, the old code returned
null (genuine miss), but the new code fell through to singleCandidate
(false-positive CALLS edge).

Root cause: resolveMemberCallByFile returns null for two semantically
different reasons — (1) type not found in the index at all, and
(2) type found but no candidate matched after narrowing. The dispatcher
treated both as "try the next fallback." The old resolveCallTarget
exited the entire function on case 2.

Fix: after the scoped resolvers both return null, check whether the
receiver type resolves in the index. If it does (case 2), null-route
— the scoped resolvers made the right decision. If it doesn't (case 1,
e.g. PHP 'mixed', dynamic types), singleCandidate is the correct last
resort. ctx.resolve is cached so the check is free.

This fixes:
- Unit: no heritageMap null-route test (was getting 1 edge, expects 0)
- Integration: Rust c.trait_only() negative test
- Integration: 3 PHP heritage + alias tests (singleCandidate correctly
  fires when the receiver type is not in the index)

Performance (findings #1, #2, #3)
- Thread pre-computed tiered result into resolveModuleAliasedCall via
  new tieredOverride parameter — eliminates the duplicate ctx.resolve
  call on every module-alias path.
- Add countCallableCandidates helper that short-circuits at threshold
  without allocating an intermediate array — replaces the
  filterCallableCandidates(...).length > 1 allocation in skipMember.
- resolveMemberCallByFile lookupCallableByName caching deferred to a
  follow-up (finding #2) — the fix requires threading widenCache
  through the file-scoped resolver which is a larger change.

Code quality (findings #4, #5)
- Remove dead code: redundant conditional in resolveMemberCallByFile
  where both branches returned null.
- Move WidenCache type declaration from mid-file (between JSDoc blocks)
  to adjacent to CONSTRUCTOR_TARGET_TYPES with other type declarations.

Formatting
- Applied prettier to call-processor.ts (CI format check was failing).

Verification
- tsc --noEmit clean
- 3188 unit tests pass (0 skipped real tests)
- 1766 resolver integration tests pass
- Zero regressions — all PHP, Rust, and no-heritageMap tests green

Review: https://github.com/abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus/pull/770#issuecomment-4225312416

* fix(SM-19): restore module-alias narrowing and constructor disambiguation

Codex adversarial review on PR #770 surfaced two silent regressions in the
SM-19 thin dispatcher:

Finding 1 [high] — Typed member calls bypassed module-alias narrowing.
When two homonym receiver types are both imported by the caller, the
import-scoped tier no longer narrows and the owner/file resolvers see
genuine ambiguity. The dispatcher null-routed silently, dropping valid
CALLS edges. Fix: consult `resolveModuleAliasedCall` at the top of the
typed-member branch so an active alias on `call.receiverName` picks the
aliased file before the generic resolvers run.

Finding 2 [medium] — Constructor dispatch lost overload disambiguation.
When `resolveStaticCall` bails (ambiguous or ownerless Constructor pool)
and the caller supplied `overloadHints` / `preComputedArgTypes`, the
branch fell straight through to `singleCandidate` — which also bails on
multiple same-arity survivors. Fix: between `resolveStaticCall` and
`singleCandidate`, run constructor-filtered overload disambiguation on
the tiered pool. Only engages when a narrowing signal is present;
preserves SM-10 R3 null-route for genuinely ambiguous cases.

Tests:
- call-processor.test.ts: 3 new dispatcher-level regression tests
  covering real-homonym alias narrowing, constructor overload
  disambiguation with `argTypes`, and null-route control
- symbol-table.test.ts: update `module alias homonyms` test which
  previously codified the Finding 1 regression; now asserts resolution
  to the aliased file's method

Verification: 3191 unit + 2398 integration tests pass; tsc --noEmit
clean; prettier clean.

* refactor(SM-19): address code review findings with clean-code pass

Code review on commit f424685e surfaced one P1 correctness regression and
two P2 maintainability concerns. This commit closes all ten findings:

P1 — Alias helper placement regression
  - resolveModuleAliasedCall now runs as a FALLBACK in the typed-member
    branch, after resolveMemberCall/resolveMemberCallByFile return null.
    Previously it short-circuited BEFORE scoped resolvers, leaking unrelated
    homonyms from the aliased file when a local var coincidentally matched
    a module alias.
  - Added type-file verification guard: alias narrowing only fires when the
    alias target file is among the receiver type's defining files. Prevents
    cross-type false positives and hardens SM-10 R3.

P2 — Thin-dispatcher drift (roadmap Phase 3)
  - Extracted disambiguateByOverloadOrArgTypes shared helper. Centralizes
    the overloadHints → preComputedArgTypes precedence rule used by both
    member and constructor resolvers.
  - Folded constructor overload disambiguation into resolveStaticCall as
    step 4.5 (between the ambiguous-pool bail and the instantiable-class
    fallback). resolveStaticCall now accepts optional overloadHints /
    preComputedArgTypes symmetric with resolveMemberCallByFile.
  - Dispatcher's constructor branch returns to a 2-line delegation.
  - resolveMemberCallByFile now calls the shared helper instead of inlining
    the ternary.

P2 — Missing test coverage
  - owner-scoped wins over alias narrowing (alias with unrelated target
    class must not override unique owner-scoped answer)
  - alias narrowing rejects unrelated target type (type-file guard)
  - alias fallthrough: receiverName not in alias map
  - alias fallthrough: alias target file has no matching method
  (overloadHints-for-constructor variant transitively covered via the
   extracted helper's member-path tests; direct dispatcher test deferred
   as it requires real OverloadHints fixture parsing)

P3 — Clarity and durability
  - Stripped "Codex SM-19 Finding N" prefixes from comments. Replaced with
    durable explanations of WHY each guarded branch exists.
  - Added cross-reference comment at the tail-branch resolveModuleAliasedCall
    call site pointing to the typed-member branch usage.

Verification: 3195 unit + 1766 resolver integration + 2398 full integration
tests pass. tsc --noEmit clean. prettier clean.

* feat: add 8 new safe output tools

Implements the remaining safe output features from GitHub issues #72-#79:

- add-pr-comment (#72): Create comment threads on Azure DevOps pull requests
  with support for general and file-specific inline comments
- link-work-items (#73): Create relationships between work items (parent/child,
  related, predecessor/successor, duplicate)
- queue-build (#74): Trigger Azure DevOps pipeline/build runs with optional
  branch and template parameters
- create-git-tag (#75): Create annotated git tags on commits in ADO repositories
- add-build-tag (#76): Add tags to Azure DevOps builds for classification
- create-branch (#77): Create new branches without immediately creating a PR
- update-pr (#78): Update PR metadata (reviewers, labels, auto-complete, vote,
  description)
- upload-attachment (#79): Upload file attachments to work items

Each tool includes:
- Params struct with validation
- Config struct for front matter configuration
- Executor with full ADO REST API implementation
- Sanitize impl for security
- Comprehensive unit tests

All 8 tools are registered in the MCP server, Stage 2 executor dispatch,
budget system, and write-permissions validation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review feedback

Bug fixes:
- create-git-tag: resolve HEAD via repository's defaultBranch instead of
  hardcoding 'heads/main' (works with master, develop, etc.)
- update-pr: resolve reviewer emails to GUIDs via VSSPS identity API
  before adding to PR (ADO reviewers endpoint requires user ID, not email)

Security:
- update-pr: require 'allowed-votes' to be explicitly configured for vote
  operation — empty list now rejects all votes to prevent accidental
  auto-approve by agents

Improvements:
- update-pr: make auto-complete merge options configurable via front matter
  (delete-source-branch, merge-strategy) instead of hardcoding squash+delete
- add-pr-comment: validate file_path in Validate::validate() at Stage 1
  (was only validated at Stage 2, giving no feedback to agent)
- queue-build: sanitize template parameter keys and values to prevent
  ##vso[] command injection in build logs

* feat: align with gh-aw — add review tools and enhancements

Adds 4 new safe output tools to align with gh-aw's PR review capabilities:

- submit-pr-review: Submit PR review with decision (approve, request-changes,

* fix: flush NDJSON file after write to prevent stale reads

tokio::fs::File::write_all may buffer data internally without
flushing to the filesystem. Under load (e.g., full test suite),
a subsequent read_to_string could see stale data, causing
write_safe_output_file_with_maximum to miss already-written
entries and exceed the configured limit.

Adding an explicit flush() ensures data reaches the filesystem
before the function returns.

commit.message:
fix(cron): 接通 CronScheduler 到 REPL 主循环

CronScheduler 实现完整但从未被启动，导致定时任务永远不触发。

- repl::run() 启动前创建 unbounded_channel
- CronScheduler::start() 在 config_watcher 之后启动，on_fire 回调将任务
  发送到 channel
- 主循环每次 readline 前用 try_recv() 排空队列，对每个触发的任务调用
  engine.submit() 并渲染输出，完成后 save_session()

限制：触发时机取决于用户下次按键（readline 阻塞期间 scheduler 可正常运行
但结果需等 readline 返回后才处理）；后续可改为 spawn_blocking + select 实现
真正的中断式触发。

feat(benchmarks): add conv_cifar_v5 suite with cutout, more filters, and hierarchical stages

Add cutout augmentation helpers (_augment_cutout, _augment_flip_cutout)
for masking random 8×8 patches as a complementary regularization
strategy to horizontal flip.

Build conv_cifar_v5 suite (12 configs) exploring five directions:
- ×20 ensembles at 20k neurons (does scaling continue past ×10?)
- More filters per branch: 48×3 (144 features) and 64×3 (192 features)
  at 20k neurons with ×5 and ×10 ensembles — cleanly testing whether
  richer convolutional features help, without GCN which hurt in v2
- Cutout augmentation (flip+cutout) as alternative to hflip-only
- Two-stage hierarchical pipelines: 64f p5→128f p3 sequential stages
  to capture higher-order patterns from first-stage feature maps
- 30k neurons with stronger regularization (alpha=2e-2) to test
  whether the 12% train-test gap can be reduced at higher capacity

Update colab_launcher.ipynb with v5 suite entry.
